item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On 8/2/14, D-82018 was sent into the mine to attempt to explore deeper into the mine.

The writing here is just sub-par. And the last addenda drops the ball really hard. Other than that, it's nice enough to deserve an upvote.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show proposed edit
- Hide

To link to a new article with notable similarities, I propose the following edit:

No miner has ever been seen to die from its injuries.

to

No miner has ever been seen to die from its injuries.1

Footnotes
1. Due to both anomalies being mines populated by anomalously durable humanoids, a possible link between SCP-2625 and SCP-3667 is under investigation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That reminds me I still need to write that "The "D" stands Dorfs" tale for project crossover.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just another average day in Dwarf Fortress…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The songs really won it for me, but the box part felt a little too obviously. Still an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We’re looking for our mine! It will be ours in time! We’ll find the ore and melt it down so we can wake the rest!

Well, that isn't at all ominous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I see the Snow White parallels like everyone else, I also couldn't help thinking of Tommyknockers while reading this. For those not in the know, Tommyknockers were creatures said to inhabit the deep recesses of mines and knocked on the walls, and occasionally whistling, too. Tommyknockers were said to be potentially dangerous, hence no miner ever wanted to see one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really thought I wasn't going to like this (I'm generally not a fan of "this thing that already exists/somebody else created/we're all familiar with…but as an SCP") but for me the horror was well-paced and just interesting enough to win me over. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really do like it, but it could use some cleanup. Tone and grammar seem off in a few places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one. Good job, Weizhong. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EH, it was all ok until the box at the end. That seems tacked in an honestly would be better with a 'hint, don't show' concept. If we're not sure what happened to the D class, I would even be inclined to think he was made into an instance of SCP-2625-1. Which honestly would be more creepy to me than the 'killed and gnawed on', specially since it would be my - theory - and not a canon thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it was terribly cute. I wanna meet the foreman. He sounds like a great guy.

item.title: Re: Guys, I found a plot hole!!
item.content:encoded: E: actually, never mind. I think they purposefully don't mention it, in the interview log.

item.title: Guys, I found a plot hole!!
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't explain what they do with the mined materials.

Do I get a cookie?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Disney, except evil."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was amazing. I went in expecting a straightforward seven dwarves SCP, but the songs (especially that second one), the implications behind what their goal is and who the foreman is, and the exploration log really knocked it out of the park. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dig dig dig up everything in sight, We got to dig dig dig, in our mine, in our mine, Dig up diamonds by the score, A thousand rubies, sometimes more, But we don't know what we are diggin' for, yeah

I found the Foreman!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this too. I would like a little more of a hint as to what the foreman really is but other than that, I have no complaints. Good show! +upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually rather liked this piece. A major part of horror is not knowing certain things. If we all knew the exact details of everything, it wouldn't be horror, it would be a description of a hazardous place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Largely echoing Smapti. The initial description didn't grab me at all, and it had me thinking "well this has to have some horrible revelation at the end." Then I got the to songs, which were just… right in your face. And then there's just no pay off. All we get is red sticky stuff and weird tunnels. The foreman doesn't do anything for as much as it's brought up unless it was the one that ate the D-Class, in which case, it's basically a big "ooga booga."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehhhhhh… it's just too much of an on-the-nose Snow White parody, and the horror just feels tacked on and leaves far too much unexplained.

item.title: Author Overview
item.content:encoded:
I kid you not, the inspiration for this was "What if Snow White was really messed up?"

Thanks to Djoric, Von Pincier, GottaGoFeast, Bennings, Abacade, Dr_Leonard, Athena_Grey, Lyricon, and many others who I've forgotten for helping me with this.
